  "text": "This analysis identifies all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2) that handle HTTP request header validation and injection prevention. The implementation spans multiple layers:\n\n## Core Header Validation & Utility Layer\n\n**source/common/http/header_utility.cc** - Implements critical header validation functions including `checkValidRequestHeaders()` which validates header names and values, and `checkHeaderNameForUnderscores()` which handles headers with underscores based on configuration (ALLOW, DROP_HEADER, or REJECT_REQUEST).\n\n**source/common/http/utility.cc** - Contains `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` which prevents HTTP request smuggling attacks by validating and sanitizing the Connection header. It prevents nomination of dangerous headers like X-Forwarded-* and pseudo-headers, enforces a maximum of 10 nominated headers, and validates TE header values.\n\n## Connection Manager & Request Processing\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc** - Implements `validateHeaders()` and `validateTrailers()` methods that invoke the header validator for request headers/trailers and handle transformation results. This is the central point where header validation is enforced at the HTTP connection manager level.\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc** - Implements `cleanInternalHeaders()` which strips internal-only headers from external requests to prevent header injection attacks. It removes Envoy-internal headers like x-envoy-decorator-operation, x-envoy-downstream-service-cluster, x-envoy-retry-on, x-envoy-force-trace, etc. Also implements `mutateRequestHeaders()` which handles various header mutations including x-forwarded-for, x-forwarded-host, and other transformations.\n\n## Protocol-Specific Validation\n\n**source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/1 codec implementation that validates headers at the protocol level, calls `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` for connection header validation, and validates header names for forbidden characters.\n\n**source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/2 codec implementation with header validation specific to HTTP/2 protocol requirements.\n\n**source/common/quic/** - QUIC stream implementations (envoy_quic_stream.h, envoy_quic_server_stream.cc, envoy_quic_utils.h) that implement header validation for QUIC protocol with methods like `validateHeader()` checking header names and values for forbidden characters.\n\n## Universal Header Validator Extension\n\n**source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/** - The Universal Header Validator (UHV) extension provides comprehensive header validation:\n\n- **header_validator.cc/h** - Base class implementing shared validation logic across HTTP versions: `validateGenericHeaderName()` and `validateGenericHeaderValue()` validate against RFC-compliant character sets, `validateMethodHeader()`, `validateStatusHeader()`, `validateSchemeHeader()`, `validateHostHeader()`, `validatePathHeaderCharacters()`, and `validateContentLengthHeader()`.\n\n- **http1_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/1 specific validator implementing `validateRequestHeaders()`, `validateResponseHeaders()`, `validateRequestTrailers()`, and `validateResponseTrailers()`. Includes special handling for Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length validation.\n\n- **http2_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/2 specific validator with protocol-specific pseudo-header validation (e.g., :method, :path, :scheme, :authority for requests; :status for responses).\n\n- **path_normalizer.cc/h** - Normalizes URL paths and validates path characters, preventing directory traversal and protocol bypass attacks.\n\n- **config.cc/h** - Factory configuration for the header validator extension.\n\n- **character_tables.h**, **config_overrides.h** - Support files for character validation and configuration override management.\n\n## Public Interfaces\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator.h** - Defines the HeaderValidator, ServerHeaderValidator, and ClientHeaderValidator interfaces that all validator implementations must follow.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_factory.h** - Defines the HeaderValidatorFactoryConfig factory interface.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_errors.h** - Defines error codes and response details for various header validation failures.\n\n## Validation Flow\n\nThe request header validation follows this flow: (1) Raw HTTP request arrives at codec layer (HTTP/1, HTTP/2, or QUIC) which performs initial protocol-specific validation. (2) Headers are then validated by the Universal Header Validator (UHV) if enabled via `validateHeaders()` in conn_manager_impl.cc. (3) `cleanInternalHeaders()` removes Envoy-internal headers from external requests. (4) `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` prevents header injection via the Connection header. (5) Route-level header transformations may add/remove headers based on configuration. This multi-layer approach ensures comprehensive defense against HTTP request header injection attacks."
}

14: namespace Envoy {

16: namespace Http {
17: namespace HeaderValidators {
18: namespace EnvoyDefault {
19: 
20: /*
21:  * Base class for all HTTP codec header validations. This class has several methods to validate
22:  * headers that are shared across multiple codec versions where the RFC guidance did not change.
23:  */
24: class HeaderValidator {
25: public:
26:   HeaderValidator(
27:       const envoy::extensions::http::header_validators::envoy_default::v3::HeaderValidatorConfig&
28:           config,
29:       ::Envoy::Http::Protocol protocol, ::Envoy::Http::HeaderValidatorStats& stats,
30:       const ConfigOverrides& config_overrides);
31:   virtual ~HeaderValidator() = default;
32: 
33:   using HeaderEntryValidationResult = ::Envoy::Http::HeaderValidator::RejectResult;
34:   using HeaderValueValidationResult = ::Envoy::Http::HeaderValidator::RejectResult;
35:   /*
36:    * Validate the :method pseudo header, honoring the restrict_http_methods configuration option.
37:    */
38:   HeaderValueValidationResult validateMethodHeader(const ::Envoy::Http::HeaderString& value);
39: 
40:   /*
41:    * Validate the :status response pseudo header based on the range of valid response statuses.
42:    */
43:   HeaderValueValidationResult validateStatusHeader(const ::Envoy::Http::HeaderString& value);
44: 
45:   /*
46:    * Validate any request or response header name.
47:    */
48:   virtual HeaderEntryValidationResult
49:   validateGenericHeaderName(const ::Envoy::Http::HeaderString& name);
50:
